final_answer: Both the Banking and Wealth segments improved sharply in 2024, but Banking outperformed Wealth on revenue scale, growth and absolute net income. Banking net income was $1.5 billion (vs. a $35 million net loss in 2023), with revenues of $6.2 billion up 32% — driven by higher Investment Banking (up 38%, to $3.6B) and Corporate Lending (up 23%), Banking expenses falling 8% to $4.5B, and a $224M cost-of-credit benefit. Wealth net income was $1.0 billion, up 139%, on revenues of $7.5 billion up 7% — driven by higher noninterest revenue (up 15%) on growth in client investment assets, expenses down 2% to $6.4B, and a $126M cost-of-credit benefit. The Segment Revenues and Income tables on page 17 confirm this: Banking income (loss) from continuing operations went from $(31)M (2023) to $1,529M (2024), while Wealth went from $419M to $1,002M (+139%). Wealth posted the larger percentage net-income gain (139% vs. NM for Banking off a loss base), but Banking produced more absolute net income ($1.5B vs. $1.0B), higher revenue growth (32% vs. 7%), and a swing from loss to profit — so Banking was the better-performing segment in 2024.

selected_chunks.content: SEGMENT REVENUES AND INCOME (LOSS). REVENUES: Banking 6,201 (2024) vs 4,715 (2023) vs 5,527 (2022), +32% YoY; Wealth 7,512 vs 7,021 vs 7,355, +7% YoY. INCOME (loss) from continuing operations: Banking $1,529M (2024) vs $(31)M (2023) vs $334M (2022), NM% change; Wealth $1,002M vs $419M vs $995M, +139% YoY.

Banking: Banking net income was $1.5 billion, compared to a net loss of $35 million in the prior year, driven by higher revenues, lower expenses and a higher benefit from cost of credit. Banking revenues of $6.2 billion increased 32%... Investment Banking revenues of $3.6 billion increased 38%... Banking expenses of $4.5 billion decreased 8%... Wealth: Wealth net income of $1.0 billion increased 139%, reflecting higher revenues, lower expenses and a higher benefit from cost of credit. Wealth revenues of $7.5 billion increased 7%, largely driven by higher noninterest revenue (up 15%)... Wealth expenses decreased 2% to $6.4 billion.
...the ACL associated with the margin lending portfolio. For additional information on the results of operations of Wealth in 2024, see 'Wealth' below.

BANKING: Banking includes Investment Banking and Corporate Lending. At Dec 31, 2024, Banking had $143B in assets, $82B in loans, $0.6B in deposits. Net interest income $2,157M (2024) vs $2,161M (2023); Investment banking fees $3,857M vs $2,713M (+42%); Total revenues $6,201M vs $4,715M (+32%); Total operating expenses $4,477M vs $4,877M (-8%).

WEALTH: Wealth includes the Private Bank, Wealth at Work and Citigold businesses. At Dec 31, 2024, Wealth had $313B in deposits, $587B in client investment assets and $148B in loans. Net new investment asset flows of $42B during 2024.

Segment operating financial summary tables: Net interest income, Non-interest revenue, Total revenues, operating expenses by Services / Markets / Banking. Banking total revenues $6,201M (2024) vs $4,715M (2023); operating expenses $4,477M vs $4,877M.
